articles.title: Nvidia’s future challenger? Chinese start-up reveals aggressive AI chip road map
articles.summary: Dongfang Suanxin, a state-backed Chinese semiconductor start-up, has unveiled a bold AI chip roadmap that leverages innovative architectures to circumvent U.S. export controls, positioning itself as a formidable contender against Nvidia in the global market. This breakthrough not only signifies a strategic shift in the semiconductor landscape but also highlights the escalating competition between the U.S. and China in advanced technology sectors.

articles.title: China works on AI safety benchmark as regulators target large model risks
articles.summary: China is developing a groundbreaking AI safety benchmark to assess the risks associated with large models, positioning itself at the forefront of global AI regulation amid increasing scrutiny from Western nations. This initiative not only aims to enhance the security of AI technologies but also sets a critical standard that could influence international practices and policies in artificial intelligence governance.

articles.title: Xi Jinping to attend World AI Conference for first time as China elevates tech push
articles.summary: President Xi Jinping's first in-person attendance at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in 2026 underscores China's strategic commitment to leveraging AI as a cornerstone for economic advancement and global leadership in technology. This pivotal moment highlights Beijing's intent to assert its influence in shaping the future of AI on the world stage.

articles.title: China’s drug industry pivots to AI-powered candidates to drive next wave of deals
articles.summary: China's drug industry is embracing artificial intelligence to identify and develop innovative drug candidates, a strategic shift that comes on the heels of a record US$110 billion in cross-border deals, positioning the country as a formidable player in the global pharmaceutical landscape. This pivot not only enhances the potential for groundbreaking therapies but also signals a transformative era in drug development that could reshape the industry worldwide.

articles.title: Patricia Evangelista on journalism
articles.summary:
• Journalism serves as a double-edged sword, revealing both the darkest and most hopeful aspects of humanity.  
• The act of documenting crimes against humanity is a profound moral responsibility that weighs heavily on the journalist's conscience.  
• Engaging with suffering and injustice can lead to a deeper understanding of the human condition and the complexities of truth.  
• The journalist's role is not just to report, but to witness and give voice to the voiceless, challenging societal indifference.  
• In the pursuit of truth, journalists navigate a precarious balance between empathy and detachment, shaping the narrative of our collective memory.

articles.title: Unmothered
articles.summary:
• The absence of a nurturing figure can leave profound gaps in our personal narratives, reshaping our identities.  
• Relationships serve as the scaffolding of our stories, and their sudden loss can lead to existential disarray.  
• The act of storytelling is not just about recounting events; it is a means of understanding and reconstructing our fragmented selves.  
• In grappling with loss, we confront the paradox of finding meaning in the very void left by those who shaped us.  
• Ultimately, the journey of self-discovery often requires us to rewrite our stories, embracing both the pain and the potential for renewal.

articles.title: China joins US in reusable orbital rocket capability
articles.summary: China's successful recovery of a reusable orbital rocket marks a significant milestone, positioning it as only the second nation after the U.S. to achieve this capability, and introduces a novel recovery technique that could reshape the future of space exploration and satellite deployment. This breakthrough not only enhances China's space ambitions but also intensifies the global competition in reusable rocket technology.

articles.title: How China plans to break SpaceX’s launch monopoly with IPO push in new space race
articles.summary: China is set to disrupt SpaceX's launch monopoly through a coordinated "wolf-pack" strategy among multiple aerospace firms, leveraging recent advancements in reusable rocketry to secure funding and expand their market presence. This initiative could significantly alter the competitive landscape of the global launch market, challenging established players like Elon Musk's SpaceX.

articles.title: China’s historic sea-based rocket recovery puts spotlight on domestic shipbuilder
articles.summary: China's successful recovery of an orbital-class rocket booster using a net-capture system marks a significant milestone in space technology, positioning the nation alongside the US and highlighting the capabilities of its domestic shipbuilder in advancing aerospace innovation. This breakthrough not only enhances China's reusable rocket program but also sets the stage for more sustainable and cost-effective space missions in the future.

articles.title: China’s optical chip breakthrough boosts AI speed 100-fold using fraction of compute power
articles.summary: Chinese researchers have achieved a groundbreaking all-optical interconnect system that enhances AI distributed inference speeds by over 100 times while consuming only one-ninth of the usual computational resources, potentially revolutionizing the industry's approach to handling the escalating demand for processing power. This innovation not only addresses the urgent need for efficiency in AI applications but also paves the way for more sustainable technological advancements.
